free_text: Differential cross-section measurements for the
proton-induced alpha and deuteron reactions and
elastic scattering on 9Be, suitable for ion beam
analysis applications
4 MV Tandem Accelerator
thin foil of 9Be target of ~200 nm
and backing of natCu of ~100 nm thickness.
Six 1500 um thick silicon surface barrier
detectors were positioned at backscattering angles of
distance of ~ 7.5 cm from the target.
The final estimated uncertainties of the cross-section
values originate from:
The beam energy uncertainty, ~2 keV, stems
from a 0.4 keV beam ripple and an average energy
straggling of the proton beam at half the target's
thickness of around 1.6 keV.
- The statistical uncertainties from the integration
of the corresponding reaction and elastic peaks.
The thickness ratio of the atomic areal
densities (3.6%)
The lateral homogeneity of the target (3%)
Proof-read by F.Maragkos (2024-02-01)
Data received from F.Maragkos for IBANDL
VT
Total uncertainties estimated (4.7-6.4%)
- Statistical uncertainties from the integration for
the elastic scattering (0.3-3.3%)
Fig.3

Total uncertainties estimated (4.9-7.0%)
